title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces · Independent C Trial
summary: An independent maintainer ports the typed callback and context registry to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of typed callback and context registry with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.
Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Function Pointer Compatibility
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 7091. Produce bounded C evidence to bind callback results to explicit context lifetime; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

Competency probe: Document nullability, bounds, aliasing, mutation, ownership, lifetime, errors, and thread rules at declarations.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Api Contract Documentation.
steps.why: Retrieving Api Contract Documentation through the concrete typed callback and context registry case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Api Contract Documentation, keep Api Contract Documentation connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document nullability, bounds, aliasing, mutation, ownership, lifetime, errors, and thread rules at declarations.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Api Contract Documentation, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Api Contract Documentation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Api Contract Documentation, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A lab native-systems team handles memory case 7282. Produce bounded C evidence to bind callback results to explicit context lifetime; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Document nullability, bounds, aliasing, mutation, ownership, lifetime, errors, and thread rules at declarations.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Api Contract Documentation, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A descriptive function name is a sufficient memory contract.
